value="<br><div><span style="white-space: pre;">	Streams, Packets, Messages and Commands</span></div><div><br><ul style="padding-left: 20px;"><li>Packets can contain Messages and Commands.</li><li>Each Packet has a sequence Id that is separate for host and zune</li><li>Commands are listed first, and are then followed by Messages</li><li>To send messages, a stream has to be opened first. Multiple streams can be opened at the same time. Each message is associated with a stream. The StreamId 0 is reserved for commands</li><li>The Zune reserves a buffer for each stream. The inital buffer size is communicated to the host via the AcceptRequest Command&nbsp;</li><li>The host has to keep track of how many bytes the Zune can consume. If the buffer is full the host has to stop sending new data. The Zune will send a DataConsumed command to tell the host that it can continue sending data</li></ul></div>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="130" width="416.25" x="353.75" y="-900" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="bPnEYzKb64ygt9mAzznM-34" edge="1" parent="1" source="bPnEYzKb64ygt9mAzznM-12" value="<pre>&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; /* Known service IDs<br>&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp;*&nbsp;<br>&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp;* {7F7534A1-293D-495E-88DA-F1EA984EF075} Profiler<br>&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp;* {92C8EE24-92DF-4cb5-99C2-B4C7068528E9} Console<br>&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp;* {7ABBE0D5-B437-42ca-B57B-CEED61680E4F} Debugger<br>&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp;* {2B80CE0A-7119-413e-BA6F-E451AC9382AF} Performance monitor<br>&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp;* {754BD393-556F-4a33-8541-BC009FFE979E} Sampling profiler<br>&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp;* {D8F4AE7C-581B-4898-97C0-F08EBC001D20} Error popup suppression<br>&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp;* {BC66BB72-DE63-4b31-956E-B3878CE9F5D2} Test target<br>&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp;* {D0F348D2-9667-4ef2-B078-BF94077177FD} Trace<br>&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp;* XnaChannelBroker&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp;Deployment/launch channel broker<br>&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp;* XnaScreenshotService&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp;Screenshot acquisition<br>&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp;* XNACHANx&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp;Brokered channel (x = returned channel ID)<br>&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp;*/<br><br><span style="white-space: pre;">	</span>- This document covers XnaChannelBroker and XNACHANx<br><span style="white-space: pre;">	</span>- A stream to a service can be opened using the RequestConnect (161) Command<br><span style="white-space: pre;">	</span>- For deploy, launch, etc first a XnaChannelBroker is used, then XNAChanx is used to send XNA Messages</pre>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="160" width="416.25" x="353.75" y="-760" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="bPnEYzKb64ygt9mAzznM-58" parent="1" style="text;html=1;whiteSpace=wrap;strokeColor=#d79b00;fillColor=#ffe6cc;align=left;verticalAlign=top;rounded=0;glass=0;fontSize=6;margin=8px;container=0;strokeWidth=1;perimeterSpacing=0;labelBorderColor=none;spacingTop=0;spacing=0;spacingLeft=0;spacingBottom=0;spacingRight=0;" value="<pre>&nbsp; &nbsp; local XNA_COMMANDS = {<br>&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; [161] = "RequestConnect",<br>&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; [162] = "AcceptRequest",<br>&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; [163] = "CancelRequest",<br>&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; [164] = "AcknowledgeAccept",<br>&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; [165] = "AcknowledgeCancel",<br>&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; [166] = "RefuseRequest",<br>&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; [177] = "Disconnect",<br>&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; [178] = "AcknowledgeDisconnect",<br>&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; [193] = "StreamClosed",<br>&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; [225] = "HostError",<br>&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; [226] = "ClientError",<br>&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; [241] = "HostRebooting",<br>&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; [209] = "KeepAlive",<br>&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; [210] = "DataConsumed",<br>&nbsp; &nbsp; }</pre>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="160" width="416.25" x="353.75" y="-590" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> </root> </mxGraphModel> </diagram> </mxfile>

ul.li: Packets can contain Messages and Commands.
Each Packet has a sequence Id that is separate for host and zune
Commands are listed first, and are then followed by Messages
To send messages, a stream has to be opened first. Multiple streams can be opened at the same time. Each message is associated with a stream. The StreamId 0 is reserved for commands
The Zune reserves a buffer for each stream. The inital buffer size is communicated to the host via the AcceptRequest Command

The host has to keep track of how many bytes the Zune can consume. If the buffer is full the host has to stop sending new data. The Zune will send a DataConsumed command to tell the host that it can continue sending data

div.pre:
/* Known service IDs
*
* {7F7534A1-293D-495E-88DA-F1EA984EF075} Profiler
* {92C8EE24-92DF-4cb5-99C2-B4C7068528E9} Console
* {7ABBE0D5-B437-42ca-B57B-CEED61680E4F} Debugger
* {2B80CE0A-7119-413e-BA6F-E451AC9382AF} Performance monitor
* {754BD393-556F-4a33-8541-BC009FFE979E} Sampling profiler
* {D8F4AE7C-581B-4898-97C0-F08EBC001D20} Error popup suppression
* {BC66BB72-DE63-4b31-956E-B3878CE9F5D2} Test target
* {D0F348D2-9667-4ef2-B078-BF94077177FD} Trace
* XnaChannelBroker Deployment/launch channel broker
* XnaScreenshotService Screenshot acquisition
* XNACHANx Brokered channel (x = returned channel ID)
*/
- This document covers XnaChannelBroker and XNACHANx
- A stream to a service can be opened using the RequestConnect (161) Command
- For deploy, launch, etc first a XnaChannelBroker is used, then XNAChanx is used to send XNA Messages
